pick.rationale: This Elite 8 rematch of last year's national championship features two nearly identical teams analytically—Florida ranks 4th and Houston 5th in both KenPom and Torvik, with both boasting elite defenses (Florida 5th, Houston 4th) and top-16 offenses. The decisive factors favor Houston: the Cougars enjoy a significant home-court advantage playing at Toyota Center in Houston, and they carry substantial motivation to avenge last year's championship loss to Florida. Kelvin Sampson's tournament pedigree—five straight Sweet 16 appearances and last year's title game run—combined with Houston's suffocating defense should neutralize Florida's balanced attack. While Florida's three All-SEC honorees and championship experience make them formidable, Houston's revenge narrative, home crowd, and elite defensive prowess give them a narrow edge in what projects as a tightly contested battle between two of college basketball's elite programs.

reasoningStep.title: South Regional Final: Florida vs Houston
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzed two top-5 teams with nearly identical analytical profiles, weighing home-court advantage, revenge motivation, and championship pedigree.
evidence: Florida ranks 4th in KenPom/Torvik (9th offense, 5th defense); Houston ranks 5th (13th-16th offense, 4th defense) - essentially even analytically
South Regional played at Toyota Center in Houston gives Cougars significant home-court advantage
Rematch of 2025 national championship where Florida defeated Houston, creating revenge motivation for the Cougars
Florida has three All-SEC honorees (Haugh, Chinyelu, Condon) but was blown out 91-74 by Vanderbilt in SEC tournament semifinals
Houston guards Milos Uzan and Emanuel Sharp playing through injuries; Florida's Thomas Haugh returned from foot injury
Kelvin Sampson has led Houston to five straight Sweet 16s; Todd Golden won last year's national championship at age 39

result.text: UH basketball: Milos Uzan, Emanuel Sharp slowed by injuries Houston Chronicle Logo Hearst Newspapers Logo Skip to main content Subscribe Local News Trending Suburbs Education Environment Health & Medicine Weather Politics Houston Texas U.S. & World Business Energy Real Estate Top Workplaces Tech Sports Texans Astros Rockets Colleges High Schools Dynamo & Dash Entertainment Lifestyle & Culture Food Restaurant Reviews Barbecue Recipes Opinion Editorials Letters to the Editor Columnists Interactives Trackers Texas Flood Map Power Outage Tracker Investigations Newsletters The 713 Texas Take Weekend Wrap Classifieds Obituaries Place an Obituary Legal Notices Place Legal Notices Place Agent/Broker Ad La Voz de Houston Visuals Puzzles Podcasts About Us Archive Newspaper Archive Chronicle Shop Privacy Notice Terms of Use Houston Chronicle Logo Subscribe Sports // College // Houston Cougars UH guards Milos Uzan, Emanuel Sharp slowed by recent injuries, Kelvin Sampson says By Joseph Duarte , Staff Writer Feb 25, 2026 UH's Emanuel Sharp and Milos Uzan have been bothered by an assortment of ailments of late, with the issues coming to a head in their two most recent games. Jason Fochtman/Houston Chronicle University of Houston senior guards Milos Uzan and Emanuel Sharp have been dealing with injuries recently, coach Kelvin Sampson said Wednesday. Sampson said Uzan has been bothered by “two really bad ankle sprains” and a foot injury that made him a game-time decision for Monday’s loss at No. 14 Kansas . Sharp, the team’s second-leading scorer, injured his hip on two hard falls in the loss to No. 4 Arizona . Advertisement Article continues below this ad Sampson said that was part of the reason he substituted both with UH down 20 and about eight minutes left against Kansas. “Those two guys were obviously less than 100%,” Sampson said. Sign up for Cougar Confidential You'll get get in-depth news and analysis about UH’s basketball and football teams from reporter Joseph Duarte by signing up for the Cougar Confidential newsletter . Sampson said team trainer John Houston was unsure whether Uzan would be able to play Monday. Houston Chronicle Logo Want more Houston Chronicle? Make us a Preferred Source on Google to see more of us when you search. Add Preferred Source “But Milos decided to give it a go,” Sampson said. “I thought he did the best he could. He just couldn’t plant or move real good on that foot.” Advertisement Article continues below this ad Neither player was listed on the Big 12 availability report prior to the game. Sampson gave his team Tuesday and Wednesday off from practice. The No. 5 Cougars will return to practice Thursday in advance of Saturday’s game against Colorado. “That’s another big reason why we gave them two days off,” Sampson said. “There’s no way Milos or Emanuel could have practiced Tuesday, and I’m pretty sure neither could practice (Wednesday).” Feb 25, 2026 Joseph Duarte UH/Big 12 Beat Writer Joseph Duarte covers University of

modelTrace.content:
This is very helpful! Now I have a clear picture:

1. **Location advantage**: The South Regional is being held at Toyota Center in Houston, giving Houston a significant home-court advantage. This is a major factor.

2. **Rematch narrative**: This is a rematch of the 2025 national championship game where Florida beat Houston. Houston is seeking redemption.

3. **Team quality**: Both teams are extremely evenly matched:
   - Florida: #4 in both KenPom and Torvik, 26-7, elite offense (9th-11th) and elite defense (5th)
   - Houston: #5 in both KenPom and Torvik, 28-5, strong offense (13th-16th) and elite defense (4th)

4. **Florida's roster**: Three All-SEC honorees - Thomas Haugh (first team), Rueben Chinyelu (first team), Alex Condon (second team). Todd Golden is SEC Coach of the Year.

5. **Injury concerns**: 
   - Florida: Thomas Haugh had a foot injury that caused him to miss a game against Mississippi State
   - Houston: Milos Uzan and Emanuel Sharp have been dealing with injuries

6. **Recent form**:
   - Florida: Lost 91-74 to Vanderbilt in SEC tournament semifinals, snapping a 12-game win streak. But they beat Vanderbilt in the Sweet 16.
   - Houston: Reached Big 12 championship game, lost to Arizona. Beat Illinois in Sweet 16.

7. **Tournament experience**: 
   - Houston: Five straight Sweet 16 appearances under Kelvin Sampson, lost in national championship last year
   - Florida: Won national championship last year under Todd Golden

The key factors:
- Houston has a significant home-court advantage playing at Toyota Center
- This is a revenge game for Houston after losing to Florida in last year's national championship


- Both teams are nearly identical in quality and defensive prowess
- Both have injury concerns with key players

The home-court advantage in Houston and the revenge motivation after last year's championship loss give the Cougars a slight edge in what should be a tightly contested game.
